filters_overview.title: Filtering Your View
filters_overview.content:
When your graph gets busy, filters help you focus! The filter panel (press F) lets you show only the nodes you care about right now.

Filters work together - enable multiple filters to narrow down exactly what you're looking for.
filters_overview.tip: Filters hide nodes visually but don't affect your actual connections - everything keeps working!

search_filter.title: Search Box
search_filter.content:
The quickest way to find something! Just start typing and nodes matching your search will be highlighted.

The search looks at:
• Node names
• Application names
• Port names

It's fuzzy, so you don't need exact matches.
search_filter.tip: Try searching for your app name (like 'Firefox' or 'Spotify') to quickly find its audio.

media_type_filters.title: Media Type Filters
media_type_filters.content:
Quick filter buttons to show only certain types of content:

🔊 Audio - Sound sources and sinks
🎬 Video - Video streams (screen sharing, cameras)
🎹 MIDI - Musical instrument data

These are your bread and butter for quickly cutting through the noise.
media_type_filters.tip: Working on music? Enable only Audio and MIDI to hide video clutter.

direction_filters.title: Direction Filters
direction_filters.content:
Filter by which way the audio flows:

➡️ Inputs - Things that receive audio (speakers, recorders)
⬅️ Outputs - Things that produce audio (mics, apps)

Useful when you're specifically looking for sources or destinations.
direction_filters.tip: Looking for where to send audio? Filter to show only Inputs.

active_filter.title: Active Only Filter
active_filter.content:
The 'Active' filter shows only nodes that are currently doing something - they have audio flowing through them.

This is great for:
• Finding what's currently making sound
• Cleaning up your view during a session
• Debugging audio issues
active_filter.tip: If you hear something but don't know where it's coming from, enable Active Only and look for the lit-up meters!

combining_filters.title: Combining Filters
combining_filters.content:
Filters combine with AND logic - a node must match ALL active filters to be shown.

For example: 'Audio' + 'Inputs' + 'Active' shows only audio input nodes that are currently receiving signal.

This lets you get very specific about what you're looking for!
combining_filters.tip: Start broad and add filters to narrow down. You can always disable filters to see more.
